uid.description: UNIX UID. If not provided, it is automatically filled with the next one available.
uid.title: uid

username.description: String used to uniquely identify the user on the server. In order to be portable across     systems, local user names must be composed of characters from the POSIX portable filename     character set (IEEE Std 1003.1-2024 section 3.265). This means alphanumeric characters,     hyphens, underscores, and periods. Usernames also may not begin with a hyphen or a period.
username.title: username

full_name.description: Comment field to provide additional information about the user account. Typically, this is     the full name of the user or a short description of a service account. There are no character set restrictions     for this field. This field is for information only.
full_name.title: full_name

userns_idmap.description:
Specifies the subuid mapping for this user. If DIRECT then the UID will be     directly mapped to all containers. Alternatively, the target UID may be     explicitly specified. If `null`, then the UID will not be mapped.

NOTE: This field will be ignored for users that have been assigned TrueNAS roles.
userns_idmap.title: userns_idmap

accepts.title: user_create
accepts.description: Configuration for creating a new user account.

id.description:
This is the API identifier for the user. Use this ID for `user.update` and `user.delete` API calls. This ID     also appears in the `users` array for each group entry in `group.query` results.

NOTE: For users from a directory service, the `id` is calculated by adding 100000000 to the `uid`. This ensures     consistent API results. You cannot change directory service accounts through TrueNAS.
id.title: id

uid.description: A non-negative integer used to identify a system user. TrueNAS uses this value for permission     checks and many other system purposes.
uid.title: uid

username.description: A string used to identify a user. Local accounts must use characters from the POSIX portable filename     character set.
username.title: username

full_name.description: Comment field to provide additional information about the user account. Typically, this is     the full name of the user or a short description of a service account. There are no character set restrictions     for this field. This field is for information only.
full_name.title: full_name

returns.title: result
returns.description: The newly created user account with password information.

user.delete.description:
Delete user `id`.

The `delete_group` option deletes the user primary group if it is not being used by
any other user.

accepts.description: ID of the user account to delete.
accepts.title: id

delete_group.description: Delete the user primary group if it is not being used by any other user.
delete_group.title: delete_group

accepts.title: options
accepts.description: Options controlling the user deletion process.

returns.description: ID of the deleted user account.
returns.title: result

user.get_instance.description:
Returns instance matching `id`. If `id` is not found, Validation error is raised.

Please see `query` method documentation for `options`.

accepts.description: Query options customize the results returned by a query method. More complete documentation with examples     are covered in the "Query methods" section of the TrueNAS API documentation.

extra.description: Extra options are defined on a per-endpoint basis and are described in the documentation for the associated     query method.
extra.title: extra

order_by.description: An array of field names describing the manner in which query results should be ordered. The field names may     also have one of more of the following special prefixes: `-` (reverse sort direction), `nulls_first:` (place     any null values at the head of the results list), `nulls_last:` (place any null values at the tail of the     results list).
order_by.title: order_by

select.description: An array of field names specifying the exact fields to include in the query return. The dot character `.`     may be used to explicitly select only subkeys of the query result.
select.title: select

count.description: Return a numeric value representing the number of items that match the specified `query-filters`.
count.title: count

get.description: Return the JSON object of the first result matching the specified `query-filters`. The query fails     if there specified `query-filters` return no results.
get.title: get

offset.description: This specifies the beginning offset of the results array. When combined with the `limit` query-option     it may be used to implement pagination of large results arrays. WARNING: some query methods provide     volatile results and the onus is on the developer to understand whether pagination is appropriate     for a particular query API method.
offset.title: offset

limit.description:
This specifies the maximum number of results matching the specified `query-filters` to return. When     combined wtih the `offset` query-option it may be used to implement pagination of large results arrays.

WARNING: Some query methods provide volatile results and the onus is on the developer to understand whether     pagination is appropriate for a particular query API method.
limit.title: limit

force_sql_filters.description: Force use of SQL for result filtering to reduce response time. May not work for all methods.
force_sql_filters.title: force_sql_filters

accepts.title: options

id.description:
This is the API identifier for the user. Use this ID for `user.update` and `user.delete` API calls. This ID     also appears in the `users` array for each group entry in `group.query` results.

NOTE: For users from a directory service, the `id` is calculated by adding 100000000 to the `uid`. This ensures     consistent API results. You cannot change directory service accounts through TrueNAS.
id.title: id

uid.description: A non-negative integer used to identify a system user. TrueNAS uses this value for permission     checks and many other system purposes.
uid.title: uid

username.description: A string used to identify a user. Local accounts must use characters from the POSIX portable filename     character set.
username.title: username

unixhash.description: Hashed password for local accounts. This value is `null` for accounts provided by directory services.
unixhash.title: unixhash

smbhash.description: NT hash of the local account password for `smb` users. This value is `null` for accounts provided by directory     services or non-SMB accounts.
smbhash.title: smbhash

home.description:
The local file system path for the user account's home directory.
Typically, this is required only if the account has shell access (local or SSH) to TrueNAS.
This is not required for accounts used only for SMB share access.
home.title: home

shell.description: Available choices can be retrieved with `user.shell_choices`.
shell.title: shell

full_name.description: Comment field to provide additional information about the user account. Typically, this is     the full name of the user or a short description of a service account. There are no character set restrictions     for this field. This field is for information only.
full_name.title: full_name

builtin.description: If `true`, the user account is an internal system account for the TrueNAS server. Typically, one should     create dedicated user accounts for access to the TrueNAS server webui and shares.
builtin.title: builtin

smb.description: The user account may be used to access SMB shares. If set to `true` then TrueNAS stores an NT hash of the     user account's password for local accounts. This feature is unavailable for local accounts when General Purpose OS     STIG compatibility mode is enabled. If set to `true` the user is automatically added to the `builtin_users`     group.
smb.title: smb

userns_idmap.description:
Specifies the subuid mapping for this user. If DIRECT then the UID will be     directly mapped to all containers. Alternatively, the target UID may be     explicitly specified. If `null`, then the UID will not be mapped.

NOTE: This field will be ignored for users that have been assigned TrueNAS roles.
userns_idmap.title: userns_idmap

group.description: The primary group of the user account.
group.title: group

groups.description: Array of additional groups to which the user belongs. NOTE: Groups are identified by their group entry `id`,     not their Unix group ID (`gid`).
groups.title: groups

password_disabled.description:
If set to `true` password authentication for the user account is disabled.

NOTE: Users with password authentication disabled may still authenticate to the TrueNAS server by other methods,     such as SSH key-based authentication.

NOTE: Password authentication is required for `smb` users.
password_disabled.title: password_disabled

ssh_password_enabled.description:
Allow the user to authenticate to the TrueNAS SSH server using a password.

WARNING: The established best practice is to use only key-based authentication for SSH servers.
ssh_password_enabled.title: ssh_password_enabled

sshpubkey.description: SSH public keys corresponding to private keys that authenticate this user to the TrueNAS SSH server.
sshpubkey.title: sshpubkey

locked.description: If set to `true` the account is locked. The account cannot be used to authenticate to the TrueNAS server.
locked.title: locked

sudo_commands.description: An array of commands the user may execute with elevated privileges. User is prompted for password     when executing any command from the array.
sudo_commands.title: sudo_commands

sudo_commands_nopasswd.description: An array of commands the user may execute with elevated privileges. User is *not* prompted for password     when executing any command from the array.
sudo_commands_nopasswd.title: sudo_commands_nopasswd

email.description: Email address of the user. If the user has the `FULL_ADMIN` role, they will receive email alerts and     notifications.
email.title: email

local.description: If `true`, the account is local to the TrueNAS server. If `false`, the account is provided by a directory     service.
local.title: local

immutable.description: If `true`, the account is system-provided and most fields related to it may not be changed.
immutable.title: immutable

twofactor_auth_configured.description: If `true`, the account has been configured for two-factor authentication. Users are prompted for a     second factor when authenticating to the TrueNAS web UI and API. They may also be prompted when signing     in to the TrueNAS SSH server using a password (depending on global two-factor authentication settings).
twofactor_auth_configured.title: twofactor_auth_configured

sid.description: The Security Identifier (SID) of the user if the account an `smb` account. The SMB server uses     this value to check share access and for other purposes.
sid.title: sid

last_password_change.description: The date of the last password change for local user accounts.
last_password_change.title: last_password_change

password_age.description: The age in days of the password for local user accounts.
password_age.title: password_age

password_history.description: This contains hashes of the ten most recent passwords used by local user accounts, and is     for enforcing password history requirements as defined in system.security.
password_history.title: password_history

password_change_required.description: Password change for local user account is required on next login.
password_change_required.title: password_change_required

roles.description: Array of roles assigned to this user's groups. Roles control administrative access to TrueNAS through     the web UI and API.
roles.title: roles

api_keys.description: Array of API key IDs associated with this user account for programmatic access.
api_keys.title: api_keys

This means alphanumeric characters,     hyphens, underscores, and periods. Usernames also may not begin with a hyphen or a period.
username.title: username

full_name.title: full_name

accepts.title: user_update
accepts.description: Updated configuration for the user account.

NOTE: For users from a directory service, the `id` is calculated by adding 100000000 to the `uid`. This ensures     consistent API results. You cannot change directory service accounts through TrueNAS.
